review_questions: Does the occurrence set distinguish large predatory species from mixed fish categories before review conclusions are drawn?
Is species-specific national advice relevant for high consumers, pregnancy-related review, or other sensitive-population communication?
Are methylmercury-driven fish and seafood contributors interpreted against the governed methylmercury TWI rather than a generic total-mercury threshold?
notes: Links EFSA mercury and methylmercury food-risk context with EU contaminants-law and official-control sampling and analysis governance.
Carries current review-oriented fish-species context for high consumers and pregnancy-related advice without implying a native species-level mercury engine.
Exposed for review-oriented provenance and monitoring context only.
Mercury occurrence support is exposed as governed EFSA dietary context linked to EU contaminants-law and official-control context.
Records include high-attention fish-species and sensitive-population metadata for review-oriented interpretation only.
Mercury occurrence records preserve fish and seafood sensitivity notes without implying a native mercury exposure engine.

Metals-occurrence record efsa.mercury.occurrence_monitoring.support: Supporting sources mix historical anchors (efsa.mercury.food.2012) with newer context records (efsa.mercury.fish_consumption.2026, efsa.metals.topic.2026, eu.reg.2023_915, eu.reg.333_2007); confirm the older evidence base is still fit for the intended use.
